_fixture_metadata.source_ref: MIP-27 §4.2, §4.7. Under Supernova, a block at nonce N contains the tx miniblock but does NOT yet carry the execution result for its own txs — the execution result is notarized in block N+k (typically N+1).
_fixture_metadata.purpose: Block N containing the DSM anchor tx. This block has NO `lastExecutionResult` for the current tx because under Supernova execution is decoupled. The dual-schema reader MUST NOT extract an ExecutionResult from this block alone — it must wait for the settling block (see block_settling_*.json).
caveats: MIP-27 §7 says `ApiBlock` preserves legacy `rootHash` top-level field during the transition window, populated from the preceding block's ExecutionResult (i.e. the state AT END of block N-1). In Supernova this is subtle: `rootHash` on block N reflects state BEFORE executing block N's txs. Audit tools that treat rootHash as 'state after this block' get stale-by-one under Supernova. This fixture deliberately populates the legacy `rootHash` field with the pre-tx state root to model this correctly. The dual-schema reader MUST prefer `lastExecutionResult` on the SETTLING block (N+1) for authoritative post-execution state, NOT `rootHash` on block N.

The block also carries a `lastExecutionResultInfo` (succinct reference) pointing to the previous block's ExecutionResult. Modeled below. Field name/shape derived from MIP-27 §B `ExecutionResultInfo`; swap on Battle Net confirmation.
